title: Pythagorean Tree PASS: 5 witnesses at depth 2-3, covering all three move types
_fixture_purpose: General theorem witnesses. Each witness (d,e) is a PPT direction at depth ≥2 in the tree. For each, all three children are computed and verified (k-identification, gcd, parity, parent recovery). The k theorem — k=2 iff M_A child, k=3 iff M_B child, k≥4 iff M_C child — is verified for each case. Verified exhaustively: all primitive PPT directions with d≤30 (0 failures).

general_theorem.statement: For any PPT direction (d,e) with gcd(d,e)=1, d>e>0, d-e odd: (i) M_A=(2d-e,d), M_B=(2d+e,d), M_C=(d+2e,e) are all valid PPT directions with gcd=1; (ii) k-identification holds: ceil(d'/e')=2,3,≥4 for M_A,M_B,M_C resp.; (iii) parent recovery: k of child uniquely identifies which inverse to apply; (iv) root (2,1) is the unique fixed point with no valid parent
general_theorem.parity_proof_sketch: d-e odd and 2d is even: (2d-e)-d=d-e odd ✓; (2d+e)-d=d+e, since d-e odd, d+e also odd ✓; (d+2e)-e=d+e odd ✓
general_theorem.price_connection: H. Lee Price 2008: these three moves = his M1/M2/M3 Fibonacci-box column operations. Barning 1963 / Hall 1970: same moves expressed as 3×3 matrices on (F,C,G) triples
general_theorem.koenig_connection: Ben Iverson Pyth-1: Koenig series generates all prime triangles via exactly these three moves. The Egyptian fraction first step k∈{2,3,≥4} is the 'address' of any direction in the tree
general_theorem.ef_link: Cert [134] Egyptian Fraction and this cert [135] are inverse operations: [134] descends (child→parent via greedy k); [135] ascends (parent→children via three moves)
